title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 8 - Frontier Town Expansion
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZZBZkqJqvu8

Daniel (@dungeondive) examines what he considers one of the greatest board game expansions ever made: the Frontier Town expansion for Shadows of Brimstone. This box transforms the settlement phase into a game within a game, adding a town board with randomized locations drawn from tokens — so every visit yields a different configuration of shops, services, and trouble.

Towns now come in small, medium, and large sizes, each with escalating travel hazards and location slots. New locations include a street market (ornate pistols, hats, horses), an Indian trading post (vision quests, tribal medicine), a mutant quarter (revolutionary contacts, mutant surgery), a gambling hall (complete with a Devil's Wheel dexterity game using the box lid), a sheriff's office (bounties, manhunts, badges for law heroes), and a smuggler's den (bank heists, cattle rustling for outlaws). A daily event deck fires each day in town, bringing everything from wealthy widows to killer-on-the-loose emergencies.

The expansion also introduces town types on a 2d6 chart — haunted towns, plague towns, mining boomtowns, outlaw towns — each layering unique events and shops. Bandits arrive as the first ranged enemies, complete with infamous gangs and a shootout mechanism that adds tactical positioning to the town board. Daniel notes he now uses the Frontier Town even with Forbidden Fortress, keeping a persistent town list via the fan-made hex crawl expansion.

----

Do you keep a persistent town list in your Shadows of Brimstone campaigns?
